text:
There was a little girl who loved apples. Every day after school she'd run home, snatch an apple from her Mom's tree, and eat it. Her favorite apple was Granny Smith.

Granny Smith lived alone. People said she was crazy. Maybe she was. But maybe she just knew how good those apples were.

Late one fall, a storm came up. Lightning cracked across the sky. An oak branch broke. Granny Smith wasn't hurt—but her favorite apple was gone!

The girl searched everywhere. Under beds. In drawers. No apple.

She sat under the oak.

Later that evening, lightning again.

That's when she saw it. A leaf. Almost lost.

She reached—

There it was!

A single Granny Smith apple.

She reached down.

And pulled it back up.

You're lucky. Think how crazy you would've seemed to an owl.

Afterwards, she brought up some baked apples.

The girl loved those. They weren't quite as good as her favorite.
